_comment: Example managed-settings.json for enterprise deployment. NOT auto-loaded. See examples/MANAGED_SETTINGS.md for the deployment story and what each key does.

permissions._comment_permissions: Managed-only `allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly: true` makes user/project allow/ask/deny rules a no-op — only this file's rules apply. Use this to enforce an org-wide policy that survives `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` and any contributor edit to .claude/settings.json.

_comment_hooks: Forces all sessions to load ONLY hooks defined here or in plugins force-enabled via managed `enabledPlugins`. Blocks user/project hooks. Combined with `allowManagedHooksOnly`, an attacker who compromises a contributor's user-level config still cannot disable the audit log or secret scanner.
_comment_strict_plugin_only: Added v2.1.129+. Blocks user/project sources from contributing skills, agents, hooks, or MCP servers — only this managed file and force-enabled plugins can. Use `true` to lock all surfaces or an array to lock only the listed ones. Combined with allowManagedHooksOnly + allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly, makes the entire customization surface admin-controlled in a fleet deployment.
_comment_parent_settings: Added v2.1.133+. Controls how parent-supplied managed settings (from embedding hosts like the desktop app) layer with this admin-deployed file. 'first-wins' drops the parent settings entirely; 'merge' applies them under the admin tier. Honored only from MDM/system tier — placing it in user/project settings is a no-op.
_comment_managed_mcp_only: When this and allowedMcpServers are both set in managed settings, only the managed allowlist applies. User/project allowlists are ignored. deniedMcpServers still merges from all sources, so users can always block more for themselves. See /en/managed-mcp for the full MCP restriction story.
_comment_skill_overrides: Added v2.1.129+. Per-skill visibility map without editing SKILL.md. Values per skill: 'on' (default), 'name-only' (skill name appears in /skills list but body doesn't auto-load), 'user-invocable-only' (only loads when user types /skill-name), 'off' (hidden). Empty object above = no overrides. Use to disable specific bundled skills your org doesn't want loaded (e.g. {"deploy": "off"}). Does not apply to plugin skills.
_comment_policy_helper: Added v2.1.136+. NOT enabled here, just documented for awareness: `policyHelper: { path: '/usr/local/bin/claude-policy' }` lets an admin-deployed executable compute managed settings dynamically at startup. Honored only from MDM/system tier. Use when settings depend on machine identity (AD group, asset tag, on-call rotation) and need to be computed per-machine instead of statically baked into this file.
_comment_deploy: Deploy this file to one of the managed-settings locations Claude Code reads BEFORE user/project settings: macOS: /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json | Linux: /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json | Windows: C:/ProgramData/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json | also (HKLM\Software\Policies\ClaudeCode registry on Windows). MDM (Jamf, Intune, etc.) is the supported delivery channel.
